email_setup_issues.title: iPhone Email Setup & Sync Problems
common_causes: Incorrect server settings after iOS update

standard_resolution_steps.details: Settings > Mail > Accounts - check if email account is listed
If 2FA enabled, must use app-specific password instead of regular password
Last resort - remove account completely, restart phone, re-add with correct settings

scam_detection.title: Identifying Scam Calls & Texts
red_flags: Urgent language ('act now', 'limited time', 'immediate action required')
Requests for passwords, SSN, or banking info via text/call
Threatening account suspension or legal action
Links to non-official domains (app1e.com vs apple.com)
Unsolicited contact claiming you won something

verification_steps.details: Real Apple messages come from specific official numbers, not random mobile numbers
Call the company directly using official phone number from their website
apple.com is real, app1e.com or appl3.com are fake. Look for subtle misspellings
Apple/banks will never ask for passwords or SSN via text or unsolicited calls

missing_apps_ios.title: Finding Missing Apps After iOS Update
common_locations: App Library (swipe left past last home screen page)
Hidden in folders or different home screen pages
Restrictions/Screen Time settings may have hidden them

resolution_steps.details: Swipe left past the last home screen to access App Library, apps auto-organized by category
Swipe down on home screen, type app name - will show if installed and where it's located
Settings > General > iPhone Storage - shows offloaded apps that can be re-downloaded
Long press app in App Library > Add to Home Screen, or drag to desired location
